programmes.show_description: The first episode of a suspenseful crime drama. A deaf woman's lipreading skills aid the police in surveilling a dangerous gang, but her bonding with a criminal risks everything.
Suspenseful crime drama. Alison's vital lipreading aids the detectives in uncovering the gang's plans, but she can only conceal her meeting with Liam from them for so long.
The New Telly: Enduring '70s British sitcom about a bus company. Stan and his family decide to buy a colour TV, but there is a problem with the disposal of their old set.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Reading Between The Lines: Comedy series about rival antique dealers. An estate agent with unseemly plans for Simon and Oliver's neighbourhood is brought to book by a lovelorn Ringo.
Olive's Divorce: Classic sitcom about a bus company. Stan takes Olive to the divorce courts as Arthur has now been gone for six months.
The Bells Are Ringing!: Classic sitcom. Mike finds his true love and announces that he is getting married, but things get complicated when Sally falls for Mike's girlfriend's father.
You're Never Too Old to Be Young: Classic '70s sitcom. Jean feels that her marriage to Sid has gone stale, but Sid is annoyed when she decides to spruce it up as a result.
Some Enchanted Evening: Classic '70s sitcom. Jean is sulking at Sid and Sally and Mike won't talk to him - when Sid finds out why, he decides to take himself off to the pub.
The Right Way to Travel: 70s sitcom. Mildred's dreams of a cheap holiday clash with George's political allegiances.
Comedy spin-off film sequel starring Harry H Corbett and Wilfrid Brambell, 1973. Albert must die when things get very bad at the Steptoes' Shepherd's Bush rag-and-bone business.
British comedy film starring Jim Dale and Kenneth Williams, 1969. A doctor tries to make a fortune selling a weight-reducing potion he found working at a mission in the South Seas.
Classic British comedy film starring Sid James and Barbara Windsor, 1972. A gang plans to raid a maternity hospital in order to steal a gigantic stock of birth control pills.
Classic British comedy film starring Sid James and Kenneth Williams, 1972. A package holiday on the island of Elsbels turns into a hilarious adventure rife with innuendo and gags.
Bawdy British comedy film starring Sid James and Barbara Windsor, 1974. 1750 - a newly-formed police force attempt to catch Dick Turpin, the most notorious of all highwaymen.

programmes.show_name: Carry On... Don't Lose Your Head
programmes.show_description: Classic British comedy film starring Sid James and Jim Dale, 1967. Two English noblemen decide to rescue their French counterparts from the revolting masses and Madame Guillotine.
Dance with the Dead: When the body of a young man is found in a Second World War-era motor car full of exhaust fumes, it looks like suicide, but all is not as it seems...
The very first episode of the detective drama series. Newly-arrived DCI McDonald and DS Dodds try to identity a man shot dead in a mansion.
A Caribbean Mystery: While holidaying on a Caribbean island, Miss Marple is drawn into a web of deceit and death, and with the help of a tycoon, sets to work unravelling the truth.
Olive's Divorce: Classic sitcom about a bus company. Stan takes Olive to the divorce courts as Arthur has now been gone for six months.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
This is ITV3+1
Definitely Not Cricket: Comedy series about two rival antique dealers. Simon and Oliver are invited to play cricket for a local team but learn that their teammates are quite mature.
The Perfect Clippie: Sitcom about a bus driver and his conductor pal. Stan is having difficulty supporting the whole family on his meagre earnings.
Debbie reveals Gennie's scheme to Pollard as jealousy about Eli's friendship with the girl overwhelms her. Lexi asks Donald for her job back and Donna tells Marlon to get a job.
Donald tells Carl about his plans to retire and asks him to be managing director of the company. Diane offers Marlon his old job back and Eli asks Gennie out for lunch.
Claire discovers the extent of the family's financial difficulties when Ashley admits to using their own money to prop up the business. Audrey makes a disturbing discovery.
Audrey's shocking discovery prompts Maria to confess her distressing secret, Marcus is suspicious when Sean returns early from holiday and Chesney is upset by Les's news.
Expiation: Feature-length drama. Lewis and Hathaway unearth a far darker case than an initial suicide verdict suggests when an Oxford housewife is found hanged in her home.
Speed Kills: Drama series. A break-in at the surgery heralds the beginning of a serious investigation for Nick, and Kate discovers the cause of a patient's previous miscarriage.
Riders of the Storm: Nick hears that a violent criminal is to be released from prison and may be heading his way, and a snowstorm leaves Kate cut off with a heavily pregnant woman.
Midsomer Rhapsody: Barnaby and Scott investigate a web of family feuds and long-lost love affairs when an inspirational retired music teacher is battered to death with a torch.
Carl is rattled when a stranger, Anna, arrives and reveals herself to be Donald's daughter. Jasmine flirts with Jake at Donald's party.
Liam struggles to come to terms with the news, as a desperate Maria seeks consolation from him. Claire has a novel idea to save money, and David is delighted by Tina's visit.
Maria and Liam finally discuss the funeral, but her worst fears seem to be realised when she sees him heading straight into Carla's arms. Gail resolves to contact Ted.
Dead Ringer: Nick is put in a difficult position when a colleague falls under suspicion for a robbery, and Kate notices Greengrass's unusual interest in a villager's greyhound.
Going Home: Nick unearths a dark secret from a local landowner's past after an attempted murder, and Kate discusses the reasons behind Dr Ferrenby's increasingly erratic behaviour.
Recovery: The discovery of a deceased addiction support worker sets Vera the challenge of investigating a deeply private woman's life.
Detective drama series. Deception and disguise plague DCI McDonald and DS Dodds as they investigate the death of a wealthy patient at a private clinic.
